title: On Writing Testable Code
body_text:
On Writing Testable Code

Testable code is not an accident; it is the residue of a boundary drawn on
purpose. When the part of a system that decides behavior is kept free of
input and output, it can be exercised exhaustively on static fixtures with
no network and no clock, and that is what makes its tests both fast and
trustworthy over the long run.

The discipline is to push the messy, side-effecting work to the edges and
leave a pure core in the middle. The core becomes a plain function: data
in, data out, the same answer every time. Everything hard to reproduce is
then concentrated in one thin layer that you mock exactly once.
author: Ada Lovelace
